event.name: JUBJUB_QUEST

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: This is the Moon (JUBJUB is here!!!)

event.text: You've discovered a small celestial body that seems to be very familar, although you're not normally this close. It's Luna, more often referred to as The Moon: Earth's only natural satellite.
Humans of this era assume Luna is uninhabited but you can see some strange mouse-like creatures wandering the surface of the planet.

choice.text: Approach the creatures and attempt to communicate.

event.text: You decide not to risk landing.

event.name: JUBJUB_MOON

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: This is the Moon (JUBJUB is here!!!)

event.text: You've discovered a small celestial body that seems to be very familar, although you're not normally this close. It's Luna, more often referred to as The Moon: Earth's only natural satellite.
Humans of this era assume Luna is uninhabited but you can see some strange mouse-like creatures wandering the surface of the planet.

choice.text: Approach the creatures and attempt to communicate.

event.text: While the creatures speak only in whistles, they seem to be able to understand you.

choice.text: (Soviet) Send your Soviet crewmember to communicate.

event.text: Your crewmember cautiously approaches and says, "Hello strrrange crrreaturrres, ve mean you niet harrrm. Ve arrre herrre to learrrn." While the creatures speak only in whistles, they seem to be able to understand you.
You decide not to communicate and return to your ship.
You ignore the moon.

event.name: CLANGER_ENCOUNTER_REAL
event.text: While the creatures speak only in whistles, they seem to be able to understand you.
The creatures listen intently. It is hard to tell, but it feels like they are empathetic. They wave at you to follow them before disappearing into their burrow.

choice.text: Follow them down into their burrow.

event.text:
You crawl down into the burrow. This is a small but cosy space filled with a plethora of strange trinkets.
The largest of the creatues greets you and offers out his hand for you to shake.

choice.text: This is too risky, we don't know what could be down there. Leave.

event.text: You return to your ship and launch into orbit.

choice.text: Ask if they have anything to sell.

event.text: The creatures nod and then wave at you to follow them before disappearing into their burrow.

choice.text: Follow them down into their burrow.

event.text:
You crawl down into the burrow. This is a small but cosy space filled with a plethora of strange trinkets.
The largest of the creatues greets you and offers out his hand for you to shake.

choice.text: This is too risky, we don't know what could be down there. Leave.

event.text: You return to your ship and launch into orbit.

choice.text: (Soviet) Explain the importance of the Abolition of Private Property.

event.text: The creatures listen intently. It is hard to tell, but it feels like they agree with the concept. They wave at you to follow them before disappearing into their burrow.

choice.text: Follow them down into their burrow.

event.text:
You crawl down into the burrow. This is a small but cosy space filled with a plethora of strange trinkets.
The largest of the creatues greets you and offers out his hand for you to shake.

choice.text: This is too risky, we don't know what could be down there. Leave.

event.text: You return to your ship and launch into orbit.

event.name: JUBJUB_RETURNS
event.text:
The creatures seem excited to see JubJub and rush over to greet you and your crew.
The leader of these creatures, who introduces himself as Major Clanger, approaches your captain and says, "We had a feeling you would arrive. It seems the Multiverse is spreading faster than we expected. I have someone I think you would like to meet."

choice.text: Follow Major Clanger to his burrow.

event.text: Once inside the burrow Major Clanger turns to you, "I would like you to meet my good friend, JubJub."

choice.text: Uh don't we already know him?

event.text: The new JubJub looks at you with his ears dipping and his smile faltering, "Well thanks I guess.."

choice.text: Oh I didn't mean it like that sorry!

event.text: The new JubJub perks up a little bit and asks to join your crew.

choice.text: I don't know what to tell you, one Jub is enough

event.text: Your crewmember looks sad and lets you know that now he has found another Jub he can't just leave them. (JubJub leaves your crew.)

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone your crew, as they are not dead.

choice.text: (JubJub) Hey friend! Don't mind the captain, they're just like that sometimes.

event.text: The new JubJub perks up a little bit and asks to join your crew.

choice.text: This is getting out of hand! Now there are two of them!

event.text:
The new JubJub looks at you, smiles, and then exlaims, "She can't do that! Shoot her or something!"
He looks at you and asks if you would mind letting him join your crew.

The new JubJub looks at you and tilts his head. "Now, that's a name I haven't heard in a long time"
He looks at you and asks if you would mind letting him join your crew.

choice.text: (JubJub) ANOTHER JUB!!! Finally after all this time!!

event.text:
The new JubJub jumps up and down and then runs over to your crewmember and they begin speaking excitedly in a language that you are unable to understand.
It seems your crewmember has invited this new JubJub onto your crew.

choice.text: This is too weird. Return to your ship.

event.text: You return to your ship.

event.name: JUB_TO_JUB_INVITE
event.text: Both JubJubs look at you.

choice.text: Oh, we don't have space for you sorry.

event.text: Your crewmember looks sad and lets you know that now he has found another Jub he can't just leave them. (JubJub Leaves your Crew.)

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone your crew, as they are not dead.

event.name: CLANGER_SHOP
event.text: The creatures walk over to a series of strange creations.

event.name: FLYING_MACHINE
event.text:
This seems to be a strange contraption that has four propellar blades that do not spin but instead move up and down.
You can't understand how this device creates lift but one of the creatures demonstrates that it does work.

choice.text: Demand they hand over the Flying Machine.

event.text: The creatures look alarmed by your tone and run off into their burrows, closing the hatches behind them.

choice.text: Take the Flying Machine back to your ship.

event.text: Your crew drag the Flying Machine back to your ship and try to figure out how it works.

choice.text: Press some buttons and try to figure out how to turn the machine on.

event.text:
You manage to turn the machine on but have no way to control it. It tears through part of your hull, damaging a system as it does so. (5 Hull Damage and Breaches)
There is nothing else to do here. The locals do not seem keen on leaving their homes.

choice.text: Give up on trying to figure out the machine and leave.

event.text: You decide to give up and launch your ship into orbit, leaving The Flying Machine and The Moon behind you.

choice.text: (Engineer Crew) Disasemble the machine to figure out how it works.

event.text:
Your crew member expertly disassembles the machine, managing to figure out how part of it works and how it is powered. Although they seem just as confused as to how it creates lift, they manage to apply what they learned to your own ship. (+2 Engine Upgrade)
There is nothing else to do here. The locals do not seem keen on leaving their homes.

choice.text: (Engi Crew) Disasemble the machine to figure out how it works.

event.text:
Your crew member expertly disassembles the machine, managing to figure out how part of it works and how it is powered. Although they seem just as confused as to how it creates lift, they manage to apply what they learned to your own ship. (+2 Engine Upgrade)
There is nothing else to do here. The locals do not seem keen on leaving their homes.

choice.text: (Shell Scientist) Disasemble the machine to figure out how it works.

event.text:
Your crew member expertly disassembles the machine, managing to figure out how part of it works and how it is powered. Although they seem just as confused as to how it creates lift, they manage to apply what they learned to your own ship. (+2 Engine Upgrade)
There is nothing else to do here. The locals do not seem keen on leaving their homes.

choice.text: (Shell Mechanic) Disasemble the machine to figure out how it works.

event.text:
Your crew member expertly disassembles the machine, managing to figure out how part of it works and how it is powered. Although they seem just as confused as to how it creates lift, they manage to apply what they learned to your own ship. (+2 Engine Upgrade)
There is nothing else to do here. The locals do not seem keen on leaving their homes.

choice.text: Follow the creatures into their burrow.
Try to shoot one of the creatures before they can make their way into their burrow.
Ask to how the Machine works.

event.text:
The creatures try their best to show you how the machine works. They seem happy to repeat themselves if you don't seem to understand. You are still unsure how it creates lift but have understood enough to be able to upgrade your own engines. (+1 Engine Upgrade)
The creatures seem to trust you and wave at you to follow them before disappearing into their burrow.

choice.text: Follow them down into their burrow.

event.text:
You crawl down into the burrow. This is a small but cosy space filled with a plethora of strange trinkets.
The largest of the creatues greets you and offers out his hand for you to shake.

choice.text: This is too risky, we don't know what could be down there. Leave.

event.text: You return to your ship and launch into orbit.

choice.text: (Engi Crew) Ask to dissemble the machine to figure out how it works

event.text:
The creatures seem unsure but they seem to trust you and allow your engineer to dissemble the machine.
Your crew member expertly disassembles the machine, managing to figure out how part of it works and how it is powered. Although they seem just as confused as to how it creates lift, they manage to apply what they learned to your own ship.
(+2 Engine Upgrade)
The creatures seem to trust you and wave at you to follow them before disappearing into their burrow.

choice.text: Follow them down into their burrow.

event.text:
You crawl down into the burrow. This is a small but cosy space filled with a plethora of strange trinkets.
The largest of the creatues greets you and offers out his hand for you to shake.

choice.text: This is too risky, we don't know what could be down there. Leave.

event.text: You return to your ship and launch into orbit.

choice.text: (Engineer Crew) Ask to dissemble the machine to figure out how it works

event.text:
The creatures seem unsure but they seem to trust you and allow your engineer to dissemble the machine.
Your crew member expertly disassembles the machine, managing to figure out how part of it works and how it is powered. Although they seem just as confused as to how it creates lift, they manage to apply what they learned to your own ship.
(+2 Engine Upgrade)
The creatures seem to trust you and wave at you to follow them before disappearing into their burrow.

choice.text: Follow them down into their burrow.

event.text:
You crawl down into the burrow. This is a small but cosy space filled with a plethora of strange trinkets.
The largest of the creatues greets you and offers out his hand for you to shake.

choice.text: This is too risky, we don't know what could be down there. Leave.

event.text: You return to your ship and launch into orbit.

choice.text: (Shell Scientist) Ask to dissemble the machine to figure out how it works

event.text:
The creatures seem unsure but they seem to trust you and allow your engineer to dissemble the machine.
Your crew member expertly disassembles the machine, managing to figure out how part of it works and how it is powered. Although they seem just as confused as to how it creates lift, they manage to apply what they learned to your own ship.
(+2 Engine Upgrade)
The creatures seem to trust you and wave at you to follow them before disappearing into their burrow.

choice.text: Follow them down into their burrow.

event.text:
You crawl down into the burrow. This is a small but cosy space filled with a plethora of strange trinkets.
The largest of the creatues greets you and offers out his hand for you to shake.

choice.text: This is too risky, we don't know what could be down there. Leave.

event.text: You return to your ship and launch into orbit.

choice.text: (Shell Mechanic) Ask to dissemble the machine to figure out how it works

event.text:
The creatures seem unsure but they seem to trust you and allow your engineer to dissemble the machine.
Your crew member expertly disassembles the machine, managing to figure out how part of it works and how it is powered. Although they seem just as confused as to how it creates lift, they manage to apply what they learned to your own ship.
(+2 Engine Upgrade)
The creatures seem to trust you and wave at you to follow them before disappearing into their burrow.

choice.text: Follow them down into their burrow.

event.text:
You crawl down into the burrow. This is a small but cosy space filled with a plethora of strange trinkets.
The largest of the creatues greets you and offers out his hand for you to shake.

choice.text: This is too risky, we don't know what could be down there. Leave.

event.text: You return to your ship and launch into orbit.

event.name: LUNAR_ROVER
event.text: This seems to be a lunar rover from Old Earth that has recently landed.

choice.text: Demand they hand over the Lunar Rover.

event.text: The creatures look alarmed by your tone and run off into their burrows, closing the hatches behind them.

choice.text: Take the Lunar Rover back to your ship.

event.text: Your crew drag the Lunar Rover back to your ship and try to see what information it might contain.

choice.text: Press some buttons and try to figure out how to turn the rover on.

event.text:
You manage to turn the rover on but have no way to control it. It tears through part of your hull, damaging a system as it does so.
(5 Hull Damage and 2 Damage to Random System)
There is nothing else to do here. The locals do not seem keen on leaving their homes.

choice.text: Decide not to risk turning the rover on and leave.

event.text: You decide to give up and launch your ship into orbit, leaving The Lunar Rover and The Moon behind you.

choice.text: (Engineer Crew) Disasemble the rover to figure out how it works.

event.text:
Your crew member expertly disassembles the rover, managing to figure out how part of it works. They make a copy of its data banks as they do so. Unforunatlety the FTL drive of your ship seems to have interfered with the Floppy Disk in the Rover and the data is corrupted.
(+1 Sensors)
There is nothing else to do here. The locals do not seem keen on leaving their homes.

choice.text: (Engineer Crew) Disasemble the rover to figure out how it works.

event.text:
Your crew member expertly disassembles the rover, managing to figure out how part of it works. They make a copy of its data banks as they do so. Unforunatlety the FTL drive of your ship seems to have interfered with the Floppy Disk in the Rover and the data is corrupted.
(+1 Sensors)
There is nothing else to do here. The locals do not seem keen on leaving their homes.

choice.text: (Engineer Crew) Disasemble the rover to figure out how it works.

event.text:
Your crew member expertly disassembles the rover, managing to figure out how part of it works. They make a copy of its data banks as they do so. Unforunatlety the FTL drive of your ship seems to have interfered with the Floppy Disk in the Rover and the data is corrupted.
(+1 Sensors)
There is nothing else to do here. The locals do not seem keen on leaving their homes.

choice.text: (Engineer Crew) Disasemble the rover to figure out how it works.

event.text:
Your crew member expertly disassembles the rover, managing to figure out how part of it works. They make a copy of its data banks as they do so. Unforunatlety the FTL drive of your ship seems to have interfered with the Floppy Disk in the Rover and the data is corrupted.
(+1 Sensors)
There is nothing else to do here. The locals do not seem keen on leaving their homes.

choice.text: Follow the creatures into their burrow.
Try to shoot one of the creatures before they can make their way into their burrow.
Ask to access the database on the Rover.

event.text:
The creatures are more than willing to assist. They plug in a hard drive and copy over any files on the Rover and hand it to you.
(Sector Map)
The creatures seem to trust you and wave at you to follow them before disappearing into their burrow.

choice.text: Follow them down into their burrow.

event.text:
You crawl down into the burrow. This is a small but cosy space filled with a plethora of strange trinkets.
The largest of the creatues greets you and offers out his hand for you to shake.

choice.text: This is too risky, we don't know what could be down there. Leave.

event.text: You return to your ship and launch into orbit.

The creatures seem unsure but they seem to trust you and allow your crew member to enter their command coeds into the rover.
The rover creates a copy of its data banks along with a schematic of its sensor array.
(+1 Sensor Upgrade, Sector Map)
The creatures seem to trust you and wave at you to follow them before disappearing into their burrow.

choice.text: Follow them down into their burrow.

event.text:
You crawl down into the burrow. This is a small but cosy space filled with a plethora of strange trinkets.
The largest of the creatues greets you and offers out his hand for you to shake.

choice.text: This is too risky, we don't know what could be down there. Leave.

event.text: You return to your ship and launch into orbit.

event.name: GREEN_SOUP
event.text: The creatues bring you over to a volcanic soup well. This must be where the soup comes from.

choice.text: Ask if you can try some soup.

event.text: The creatures whistle excitedly and offer you a bowl of soup.
You drink the soup and an energy fills you. You feel youthful again. You feel alive.

choice.text: Ask if you can take some soup back to your ship.

event.text:
The creatues whistle excitedly and help you bring back a large supply of soup to your ship.
(Soup Augment)
The creatures seem glad that you have taken the time to learn about their soup and they wave for you to follow them before disappearing into their burrow.

choice.text: Follow them down into their burrow.

event.text:
You crawl down into the burrow. This is a small but cosy space filled with a plethora of strange trinkets.
The largest of the creatues greets you and offers out his hand for you to shake.

choice.text: This is too risky, we don't know what could be down there. Leave.

event.text: You return to your ship and launch into orbit.

choice.text: Actually this isn't a good idea. Don't drink the soup.

event.text: The creatures understand your hesistation but seem glad that you have taken the time to learn about their soup and they wave for you to follow them before disappearing into their burrow.

choice.text: Follow them down into their burrow.

event.text:
You crawl down into the burrow. This is a small but cosy space filled with a plethora of strange trinkets.
The largest of the creatues greets you and offers out his hand for you to shake.

choice.text: This is too risky, we don't know what could be down there. Leave.

event.text: You return to your ship and launch into orbit.

choice.text: Thank the creatures for their time.

event.text: The creatures seem glad that you have taken the time to learn about their soup and they wave for you to follow them before disappearing into their burrow.

choice.text: Follow them down into their burrow.

event.text:
You crawl down into the burrow. This is a small but cosy space filled with a plethora of strange trinkets.
The largest of the creatues greets you and offers out his hand for you to shake.

choice.text: This is too risky, we don't know what could be down there. Leave.

event.text: You return to your ship and launch into orbit.

event.name: CLANGER_ATTACK
event.text:
Your shot hits one of the creatures who falls down. The remaining creatures drag the fallen creature into the burrow and close the hatches. Your radio picks up a distress signal coming from the burrows. It sounds like backup is on its way.
You hurry back to your ship but it is too late. There is a gathering ship already in Orbit.
(ASB Activated)
Prepare for a fight.

event.name: CLANGER_BURROW_CLOSED
event.text: By the time you reach their burrow they have closed their hatches.
You hear whistling from inside.

The whistling inside instensifies before you hear bolts click and the hatch open.
The largest of the creatues greets you and offers out his hand for you to shake. He seems to forgive you.

choice.text: Bang on the hatch even harder.

event.text:
The noises inside stop.
The hatch remains inpenetrable.
As you return to your ship you notice that the door has been pryed open.

choice.text: I'm sure it's nothing. Take off.

event.text:
As you take off you notice a faint ticking sound before a massive explosion rocks your ship.
(10 hull damage)
After patching the hole in your hull you find a note that reads, "Don't mess with my friends!! - JubJub"
There is no visible damage to your ship.

As you take off you notice a faint ticking sound before a massive explosion rocks your ship.
(10 hull damage)
After patching the hole in your hull you find a note that reads, "Don't mess with my friends!! - JubJub"

Your crew can sense another telepath nearby but can't work out where they are or what kind of creature they are. Your crew member suddenly cries out in pain and refuses to use their telepathy while you remain on this planet.

As you take off you notice a faint ticking sound before a massive explosion rocks your ship.
(10 hull damage)
After patching the hole in your hull you find a note that reads, "Don't mess with my friends!! - JubJub"

Your crew can sense another telepath nearby but can't work out where they are or what kind of creature they are. Your crew member suddenly cries out in pain and refuses to use their telepathy while you remain on this planet.

As you take off you notice a faint ticking sound before a massive explosion rocks your ship.
(10 hull damage)
After patching the hole in your hull you find a note that reads, "Don't mess with my friends!! - JubJub"

choice.text: This is a waste of your time. Return to your ship.

event.text: You make your way back to your ship and take off into orbit.

event.name: JUB_ENCOUNTER
event.text:
The creature pulls out a strange deivce which crackles to life. After a couple of seconds the device begins to translate the strange whistling sounds.
"Hello there."
"I am Major Clanger, it is a pleasure to meet you. Is this the first time you have visited here? I can't keep track of which multiverse we're in. Anyway, sorry, I'm rambling. You're on an important mission."
"If you'd follow me I have someone I'd like you to meet."

He leads you down a corridor and into a much tidier burrow filled with tech more advanced than you've ever seen before.
A definitely-regular-sized Dinosaur pokes his head out of the crate he was rummaging in.
"Oh Hello! It's Me! JubJub! It's nice to meet you."

choice.text: A dinosaur? In space? How.. what.. how did you get here?

event.text: Oh it's a long story. I can tell you if you'd like?

So I'm much older than you might realise; I was born in regular dinosaur times. We had a very advanced soviety where everyone was equal, the main concept was "Do what you can, get what you need." And it was great! There was no war or hunger or poverty. We had started sending out rockets to different planets to explore and see who we could meet. Our closest Celestial Body (I beleive you call this The Moon) was our first mission. This was relatively simple to reach but once we realised that it was already inhabited we had no right to claim the space as ours and focused our space missions elsewhere. There was, howeveer, a need for a liason between the people living here (They're called Clangers by the way) and the dinosaurs. I volunteered to be the Ambassador to The Moon. I was sent up in a shuttle and spent time getting to know the Clangers and other inhabitants here. It's really quite cosy here.

There was unforunately.. an incident on Earth. As a peaceful species, we didn't have any need for particularly advanced weapons or defence systems. So when a large asteroid had Earth in its path, we were powerless to stop it. I watched from here as all of my friends and family were destroyed by the strike.
I miss them.

But I have made a life for myself here. I have friends here and I am glad to have company. One of my good friends here is called the Soup Dragon. She isn't here at the moment, she's on a long-term expedition down towards the core of The Moon. But she has this amazing soup which has extended my lifespan. This is why I have managed to live to be millions of years old.
(He scratches his belly with his foot)

choice.text: Oh maybe some other time, I'm kind of in a rush right now.

event.text: No worries! Let me just explain what was supposed to happen.

choice.text: Hey I am not going anywhere buckko!

event.text: "Oh, no worries. It was lovely to meet you. I have a restock of supplies for you. Good luck on your mission" (+20 Scrap, +4 Fuel, +2 Missiles, +2 Drone Parts)
You decide to return to your ship.

choice.text: We don't have time, our mission requires our full attention.

event.text: "Oh, no worries. It was lovely to meet you. I have a restock of supplies for you. Good luck on your mission" (+20 Scrap, +4 Fuel, +2 Missiles, +2 Drone Parts)
You decide to return to your ship.

event.name: JUB_ENCOUNTER_CONTINUED
event.text: In the original Earth timeline the USSR landed on the Moon in 1968 with their Zond-5 craft which was manned by two cosmonauts. I greeted them and they agreed to take me back to Earth with them. The leader of the Soviet Union at the time Leonid Brezhnev was keen to take me on as an Ambassador to the USSR from the Dinoasurs (and also The Moon). The Zond-5 mission was then classified and records were changed to suggest it was an unmanned mission to circle the moon in a circumlunar trajectory. No mention of landing or of my existance was ever mentioned officially. So far as records show, the USSR never landed on the moon. I worked with Brezhnev, Andropov, Chernenko, and Gorbachev. I worked to assist with the aftermath and cleanup of the Chernobyl Disaster. I also worked to help train many Soviets in various different techniques that I had developed over the years. I proved an effective ally.

But as we know, the multiverse is a strange place. Timelines change, things happen. And here you are. A whole two days before the Soviets would fix their engines and land on The Moon. In order to keep some of the timeline consistency, and so Earth in this multiverse does not completely fall to Capitalism, I ask that you help me to return to Earth?

choice.text: (Soviet) "We know the landing coordinates, we can help you! Welcome aboard Comrade!"

event.text: The Clangers give you a gift as you return to your ship. They all stand and wave goodbye to JubJub. (+20 Scrap, +4 Fuel, +2 Missiles, +2 Drone Parts)
You decide to return to your ship.

choice.text: I don't think we have time for that. You're welcome to join our crew though!

event.text: I guess if the Rebels win then Earth will fall anyway. It'll be kind of like I'm still helping I guess.. Sure, I'll join your crew. (JubJub joins your crew)
The Clangers give you a gift as you return to your ship. They all stand and wave goodbye to JubJub. (+20 Scrap, +4 Fuel, +2 Missiles, +2 Drone Parts)
You decide to return to your ship.
The Clangers give you a gift as you return to your ship. (+20 Scrap, +4 Fuel, +2 Missiles, +2 Drone Parts)
You decide to return to your ship.

event.name: FIND_SOVIET_SHIP

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: A strange flashing light shows up on your sensors here

event.text: You arrive at the beacon looking for the distress call. It takes you a minute to realise the call is coming from a small shuttle that looks like something out of a history book.

It appears that the ship does not have a two-way video link but connects to an audio line.
Your translator flags the language as an Old Earth language, Russian.
"Hello? Kan you help us? Ve arrre having engine trrrouble."

choice.text: Ignore their request for help and leave.

event.text: You turn your attention back to your own ship.

choice.text: (Slug) Mimic their accent and offer to assist them. "Aw, someone having engine trrrouble? Ve arrre happy to help"

event.text: "You sound like Amerrrikan spy.."

The voice on the other end of the line shouts "You vill never steal ourrr secrrrets!" before their ship explodes, leaving nothing but a few pieces of scrap.
(+10 Scrap)
The voice on the other line seems to relax a little, they seem convinced.

choice.text: (Slug) Ahaha, I bet Amerrrikans haven't made it to space yet. Ve arrre herrre to help.

event.text: The voice on the other end of the line seems to relax a little.
The ship appears to be an Old Earth Spacecraft with crew from that era. They have no FTL drive and are working with a simple rocket engine.

choice.text: Ignore their request for help and leave.

event.text: You turn your attention back to your own ship.

choice.text: Try to steal some scrap from the hull of their ship, it doesn't seem like their sensors would pick you up.

event.text:
Their hull is very fragile, attempting to collect scrap caused their ship to collapse.
(+15 Scrap)
"You.. arrre us..? Frrrom another time? Zen you know ourrr strrruggles, please help us!"

choice.text: "We don't have time for this, we've already helped you once."

event.text: "I see yourrr goodvill has its limits.. verrry vell Kaptain"
You turn your attention back to your own ship
You turn your attention back to your own ship.

event.name: SOVIET_SHIP_HELP
event.text: "Ve arrre on mission to land on moon. If you vouldn't mind helping us fix ourrr engines ve vill be on ourrr vay."

You help them repair their engines and they continue their tradjectory towards the moon.
They offer you some scrap in return.
(+20 Scrap)

choice.text: You are welcome to join our crew if you'd like, we can take you to the moon?

event.text: "Zat verrry kind offer. But how vould ve rrreturrrn frrrom moon vithout ourrr rrrocket?"

choice.text: You could keep travelling with us afterwards if you would like?

event.text:
"Perrrhaps ve vill learrrn many new zings to brrring back to USSRRR. Ve agrrree."
"One of us vill kome vith you, other vill pilot rrrocket back to Earrrth, it too valuable to leave forrr Amerrrikans to kapturrre."
(Soviet joins your Crew)

choice.text: We can take you back to Earth afterwards.

event.text:
"Zis sounds like good deal. I beginning to like you Kaptain.
One of us vill kome vith you, other vill pilot rrrocket back to Earrrth, it too valuable to leave forrr Amerrrikans to kapturrre."
(Soviet joins your Crew)

choice.text: (JubJub) I am hoping to return to Earth to assit Brezhnev. Would I be able to join you?

event.text: "Ve vould value yourrr serrrvice to ourrr nation."

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone your crew, as they are not dead.

choice.text: (JubJub) I am hoping to return to Earth to assit Brezhnev. We can take you to Earth if you would like?

event.text:
"Fellow Komrrrade, you speak of zings you should not know. I feel strrrange konnection to you. I vill accept zis offer."
"Ve must take ourrr rrrocket vith us, it too valuable to fall into hands of Amerrrikans"

choice.text: We can destroy the ship if you would like, leave no trace?

event.text: "Zis bad plan, it make mission look like failurrre. But I apprrreciate yourrr offer"

choice.text: We can tow your ship with us if you would like, you will be safer on board our ship.

event.text: "Zis sounds like good plan. Successful rrreturrrn key forrr Soviet image. Ve vill both join you on yourrr ship"

choice.text: (Use your Mine Launcher. [-5}]) We can set a trap for the Americans. If they try to recover your ship it will explode.

event.text: "You arrre zinking rrright Komrrrade, you arrre zinking rrright. I like zis plan. Brrrezhnev vill be most pleased."

event.name: JUBJUB_COMRADE
event.text: "Komrrrade? You arrre Soviet?"

choice.text: I'm not a Soviet! How dare you!

event.text:
JubJub looks at you dissaprovingly as the ship responds
"Sorrry, ve did not mean to offend you"
JubJub walks over to you, "You didn't have to do that! I can't continue to work with you." He then proceeds to put on his space suit before hopping in the airlock.

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone your crew, as they are not dead.

event.text: You turn your attention back to your own ship.

choice.text: We are froma different time but we are happy to help!
(JubJub) In an uncanny Sean Connory accent, "Give me a ping, Vasily. One ping only, please"

event.text: "You know Vasily, legendarrry submarrrine sonarrr officer?"

choice.text: I have no idea what you're talking about but we're happy to help.

(JubJub) "Once more, we play our dangerous game, a game of chess against our old adversary - The American Navy. For forty years, your fathers before you and your older brothers played this game and played it well. But today the game is different. We have the advantage"

event.text:
You hear laughter from the other end of the line.
"I like zis one, you arrre funny."

event.name: JUB_EARTH_QUEST
event.text: As you approach the planet that is all too familiar to you from the history books, you hear a slow but rhythmic beeping from your radio.

choice.text: Approach the surface of the planet.

event.text:
(Scene change to surface of the Earth) As you enter Earth's atmosphere it becomes clear that this is not the Earth that you have learned about. There is something missing. The Federation shipyards do not exist, the technology that you deem essential does not seem to exist. This is Earth from 1968.
As you approach the coordinates that the Soviet's provided, your radio crackles to life:
"Вы находитесь в воздушном пространстве СССР, пожалуйста, обоснуйте своё присутствие."

choice.text: Tap your translator to see if it does anything.

event.text: Komrrrade? Ve do not rrrekognise you. Please land in designated arrrea and ve vill grrreet you in perrrson.

Your translator kicks in.
"Ah, velkome back! Ve have been vaiting to hearrr frrrom you. Please land in designated arrrea and ve vill grrreet you in perrrson."

choice.text: (Sputnik) We found this sattelite, does it belong to you?

event.text: You turn your attention back to your own ship.
You turn your attention back to your own ship.

event.name: JUB_TRANSLATED_MESSAGE
event.text:
Your translator kicks in as the voice repeats its message,
"You arrre in USSR airrrspace, please justify yourrr existence."

choice.text: We mean you no harm. We are here to return your crew.

event.text: Zis good news. Please land in designated arrrea and ve vill grrreet you in perrrson.

choice.text: That is none of your business.

event.text: The comms shut off and you hear warning sounds as several missiles head in your direction.

choice.text: Ignore the message and return to orbit.

event.text: You turn your attention back to your own ship.

event.name: JUB_EARTH_LANDING
event.text:
As you land you see a line of cars with small red flags with a Hammer and Sickle flying at the front.
You are invited into the cars and driven to a large fortified complex. Inside you are greeted by none other than Leonid Brezhnev himself.
"Greetings Comrades. I am very pleased to meet you. I hope you have travelled well?"

Brezhnev listens intently before saying, "That does indeed sound like quite the task. I wish you the very best."
"While you are out there, saving the.. Multiverse?, would you mind helping to spread and share the Communist Ideology?"

choice.text: "We would be more than happy to!"

event.text: "I am very glad to hear it! Feel free to stay as long as you like."
You return to your ship.

choice.text: I will do no such thing!

event.text: "No worries, I welcome your company all the same. Feel free to stay as long as you like."
You return to your ship.
Ah, a Comrade of few words. I can respect that. Feel free to stay as long as you like.
You return to your ship.
Ah, a Comrade of few words. I can respect that. Feel free to stay as long as you like.
You return to your ship.

choice.text: (JubJub) JubJub comes forward and explains his story.

event.text:
"Ah! A fellow Comrade! Welcome! We have much to discuss. If you wouldn't mind staying a while we would really value your support.
JubJub looks to you for approval.
JubJub looks at you gratefully before saying, "Thank you dear friend. This action will not be forgotten. To aid you in your mission, take these blueprints to my ship. I have no need for them now."

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone your crew, as they are not dead.

event.text: You return to your ship.

choice.text: "There's too much at stake, we need you."

event.text: JubJub looks a little sad but says, "I understand, Friend. The rebellion must be quashed!"
You return to your ship.

event.name: JUB_EARTH_SPUTNIK
event.text: Your translator kicks in, "Ah da! Zat ourrrs. Please rrreturrrn it to us.

"Zank you! Take schematics forrr yourrr trrrouble."
(Added Sputnik Drone)
"Zis not rrrequest, rrreturrrn sattelite now orrr face konsequences."

"Zank you! Take schematics forrr yourrr trrrouble."
(Added Sputnik Drone)
The comms shut off and you hear warning sounds as several missiles head in your direction.

event.name: JUB_SPACE_SPUTNIK
event.text: You come across a strange sattelite floating in space.
You add the strange sattelite to your hold
